frameworks.name: drift-diffusion
frameworks.one_line: single-trial LIP population signal as deterministic drift plus stochastic diffusion

frameworks.name: bounded evidence accumulation
frameworks.one_line: noisy evidence integrated to a termination bound by parietal neurons

frameworks.name: population coding
frameworks.one_line: scalar decision variable read out as weighted sum across LIP neurons

core_question: Can a single-trial drift-diffusion decision variable be directly observed in LIP population activity and shown to drive choice and RT variability?
key_assumptions: LIP carries the accumulator signal for motion-direction decisions
decision variability arises from a stochastic component of evidence accumulation
a linear weighting of population activity yields the latent decision variable
manipulations: simultaneous recording from hundreds of LIP neurons during single decisions
weighting subsets of neurons by response-field overlap with choice targets
key_findings: a scalar derived from weighted LIP population activity behaves as drift plus diffusion on single trials
this single-trial signal predicts both choice and reaction-time variability
the decision variable is carried by a small subset of LIP neurons whose response fields overlap the choice targets
these neurons integrate noisy evidence from direction-selective neurons within LIP itself
interpretations: single-trial DDM-like dynamics, long inferred from trial averages, are directly observable in LIP
parsimonious within-LIP integration architecture would be missed by hypothesis-free state-space analyses
limitations_noted: the within-LIP integration architecture is only detectable given a clear prior hypothesis
